---
title: "Harmonic Analysis 02-27-19"
output: 
  html_document: 
    toc: false
    toc_float: true
    theme: cosmo
    css: style.css
---

\newcommand{\C}{\mathbb{C}}
\newcommand{\Z}{\mathbb{Z}}
\newcommand{\N}{\mathbb{N}}
\newcommand{\R}{\mathbb{R}}
\newcommand{\<}{\left<}
\newcommand{\>}{\right>}
\newcommand{\ra}{\rightarrow}

# Plancherel's Formula

Some properties of Schwars functions: $f,g\in S(\R)$

  i) $f*g\in S(\R)$
  ii) $f*g=g*f$
  iii) $\mathcal{F}(f*g)=\mathcal{F}(f)\mathcal{F}(g)$
  
_Proof:_ (of iii))

$$\mathcal{F}(f*g)=\int_\R f(y)\int_\R g(x-y)e^{-2\pi i x\gamma}dxdy$$

$$=\int_\R f(y)\hat g(\gamma)e^{-2\pi i y\gamma}dy=\hat f(\gamma)\hat g(\gamma). \ \square$$

The Schwarz space can be equipped with the inner product inherited from $\mathscr L^2(\R)$

$$\<f,g\>\equiv\int_\R f(x)\bar g(x)dx$$

and hence $\|f\|\equiv\sqrt{\<f,f\>}=\sqrt{\int_\R|f(x)|^2dx}.$

- Remark: Compare with Parseval's equility of Fourier series:

$$\|f\|_2=\sqrt{\sum_{n\in\Z}|\hat f(n)|^2}$$

## _Plancherel's theorem:_ 

If $f\in S(\R)$ then 

$$\|\hat f\|=\|f\|.$$

_Proof:_ Define $\tilde f(x)=\bar f(-x)$. Then

$$\mathcal{F}(\tilde f)=\int_\R f(-x)e^{-2\pi i x \gamma}dx$$

$$=\overline{\int_\R f(-x)e^{2\pi i x\gamma}dx}$$

$$=-\overline{\int_\infty^{-\infty} f(y)e^{-2\pi i y \gamma}dy}$$

$$=\overline{\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} f(y)e^{-2\pi i y \gamma}dy}=\overline{\mathcal{F}(\gamma)}.$$

Let $h=f*\tilde f$. Then 

$$\mathcal{F}(h)=\mathcal{F}(f)\mathcal{F}(\hat f)=\mathcal{F}(f)\overline{\mathcal{F}(f)}.$$

Also, 

$$h(0)=f*\tilde f(0)=\int_\R f(0-t)\tilde f(t)dt=\int_\R f(-t)\tilde f(t)dy$$

$$=\int_\R f(-t)\overline{f(-t)}dt=\|f\|^2.$$

By the inversion theorem:

$$h(0)=\int_\R\hat h(\gamma)e^{2\pi i \gamma 0}d\gamma=\int_\R\hat h(\gamma)d\gamma$$

$$=\int_\R|\hat f(\gamma)|^2d\gamma=\|\hat f\|^2. \ \square$$

_Example:_ Solving differential equations

$$f^{(3)}(x)+3f''(x)-2f'(x)-6f(x)=e^{-\pi x^2}$$

$$\mathcal{F}\left\{f^{(3)}(x)+3f''(x)-2f'(x)-6f(x)\right\}=\mathcal{F}\left\{e^{-\pi x^2}\right\}$$

$$\downarrow$$

$$\hat f(\gamma)\left((2\pi i \gamma)^3+3(2\pi i \gamma)^2-2(2\pi i\gamma)-6\right)=e^{-\pi x^2}$$

Set $t=2\pi i\gamma$

$$\hat f(\gamma)(t+3)(t^2-2)=e^{-\pi x^2}\implies\hat f(\gamma)=\frac{e^{-\pi x^2}}{(t+3)(t^2-2)}$$

Note that $(t+3)(t^2-2)$ does not have purely real roots. Hence, we can invert the above to find

$$f(x)=\mathcal{F}^*\left\{\frac{e^{-\pi x^2}}{(t+3)(t^2-2)}\right\}.$$
